status: TEMPLATE — replace ALL placeholder values before activation. Do not use this file unmodified.
instructions: 1. Fill every <REPLACE:...> value with your tenant-specific data.
2. Set each feature_flag to true only if the capability is licensed, integrated, and confirmed reachable in your tenant.
3. Complete the query_field_validation section by running each field name against your XDR Search App schema before your first activation.
4. Confirm each permission in required_permissions is granted to the operator account that will run this playbook.
5. Rename this file to tenant_manifest.<your-tenant-id>.json after completing it — keep the template untouched.

used_by: Q-002 — filters out internal IPs from outbound connection results
INTERNAL_IP_RANGES.validation_note: Must cover all RFC-1918 and any private-range IPs in use on your estate. Gaps here cause Q-002 to return internal IPs as false-positive external connections.

used_by: C-002 — sets TTL on all Suspicious Object Management entries
SOL_TTL_DAYS.validation_note: Set per your organization's threat intelligence retention policy. Must exceed MONITORING_WINDOW_HOURS / 24 to avoid IOC expiry during the monitoring window.

MONITORING_WINDOW_HOURS.validation_note: Minimum recommended value is 72 hours (3 days). Must be less than SOL_TTL_DAYS * 24.

APPROVAL_IR_LEAD.value: <REPLACE: IR Lead identity — email, username, or phone>
used_by: APR-001 — contact for approval of C-001, C-004, R-001
APPROVAL_IR_LEAD.validation_note: This person receives APR-001 requests. Must be reachable at incident time. Confirm out-of-hours escalation path is in place.

APPROVAL_IR_MANAGER.value: <REPLACE: IR Manager identity — email, username, or phone>
used_by: APR-001 — escalation target when APPROVAL_TIMEOUT_MINUTES elapses without approval
APPROVAL_IR_MANAGER.validation_note: This person is the escalation target only. Does not approve APR-001 — approval remains with IR Lead. On timeout, escalate here and wait.

used_by: APR-001 — triggers escalation to APPROVAL_IR_MANAGER when elapsed
APPROVAL_TIMEOUT_MINUTES.validation_note: 30 minutes is the recommended default. Set lower for high-severity incidents. Timeout triggers escalation only — it never auto-approves.

PATCH_TEAM_CONTACT.value: <REPLACE: patch team contact — email, ticket queue, Slack channel, or phone>
used_by: EH-001 — CVE patch deployment handoff at E-004
PATCH_TEAM_CONTACT.validation_note: Must be the channel through which patch team accepts and acknowledges incident-driven patching requests. Confirm they can receive out-of-hours requests.

used_by: EH-001 — SLA timer for patch deployment confirmation
PATCH_TEAM_SLA_HOURS.validation_note: R-001 is hard-blocked until EH-001 confirmation is received. If patch SLA exceeds your endpoint isolation tolerance, pre-negotiate a faster SLA for active incidents.

FIREWALL_TEAM_CONTACT.value: <REPLACE: network security team contact — email, ticket queue, or phone>
used_by: EH-002 — perimeter firewall IP block handoff at C-006
FIREWALL_TEAM_CONTACT.validation_note: Must be the channel through which perimeter firewall rules are requested and confirmed. Required when MALICIOUS_IPS is non-empty.

used_by: EH-002 — SLA timer for perimeter firewall rule confirmation
FIREWALL_TEAM_SLA_HOURS.validation_note: R-001 is blocked until EH-002 confirmation received. Must be shorter than total expected containment duration.

BACKUP_TEAM_CONTACT.value: <REPLACE: backup and recovery team contact — email or phone>
used_by: EH-003 — conditional backup restore handoff at R-005
BACKUP_TEAM_CONTACT.validation_note: This handoff is conditional — only activated if E-002 fails or endpoint integrity cannot be confirmed. Confirm contact even if you do not expect to need it.

IDENTITY_TEAM_CONTACT.value: <REPLACE: identity and directory services team contact — email or phone>
used_by: EH-004 — account reset fallback when FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY = false
IDENTITY_TEAM_CONTACT.validation_note: Only activated when FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY = false or IDENTITY_INTEGRATION_TYPE = none. If FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY = true, RA-007 handles account reset natively and this contact is standby only.

POST_INCIDENT_REPORT_OWNER.value: <REPLACE: post-incident report distribution owner — email or name>
used_by: EH-005 — post-incident report distribution at PI-004
POST_INCIDENT_REPORT_OWNER.validation_note: This person receives the full evidence package and is responsible for management report distribution and IR documentation update.

FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY.set_true_if: Identity Security integration is configured, IDENTITY_INTEGRATION_TYPE is not none, and integration status = Connected in Vision One
FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY.fallback_if_false: EH-004 activates — C-004 account reset becomes an external handoff to identity team

FEATURE_ASRM.set_true_if: Attack Surface Risk Management is licensed and asset data is populated for affected endpoint types
FEATURE_ASRM.fallback_if_false: Skip T-001 and PI-002 — document risk prioritization and estate exposure assessment gaps

FEATURE_SANDBOX.set_true_if: Threat Analysis Center is enabled in tenant and SANDBOX_QUOTA_REMAINING > 0
FEATURE_SANDBOX.fallback_if_false: Skip T-004 — proceed directly to T-005 Threat Intelligence lookup. Document that behavioral detonation was not performed.

FEATURE_NETWORK_SENSOR.set_true_if: A network sensor is deployed and feeding telemetry to this Vision One tenant
FEATURE_NETWORK_SENSOR.fallback_if_false: Q-002 uses endpoint-reported network telemetry only — coverage may be incomplete for server-class endpoints. Document telemetry scope.

FEATURE_DETECTION_MODEL_MGMT.set_true_if: Detection Model Management edit access (not read-only) is confirmed for the operator account
FEATURE_DETECTION_MODEL_MGMT.fallback_if_false: Skip D-002 and PI-001 — document detection coverage gap and escalate tuning recommendation to tenant administrator

SENSOR_ENDPOINT_TYPE.validation_note: Used to validate telemetry field availability. Some event types and field names differ across sensor types. Confirm with the query_field_validation section.

IDENTITY_INTEGRATION_TYPE.validation_note: Set to none if FEATURE_IDENTITY_SECURITY = false. If hybrid: confirm both Azure AD token revocation and on-prem session termination are in scope for RA-007.

SANDBOX_QUOTA_REMAINING.validation_note: Check current quota in Threat Analysis Center before each playbook activation if FEATURE_SANDBOX = true. A value of 0 overrides FEATURE_SANDBOX for the T-004 step — treat as false.

required_permissions.conditional: Only required if FEATURE_DETECTION_MODEL_MGMT = true. Read-only access is insufficient for PI-001 tuning.

query_field_validation.instructions: Before first playbook activation, validate each field name in your tenant's XDR Search App schema. Enter your confirmed tenant-local field name in the 'tenant_field_name' key. Mark 'validated' as true only after confirmed with a live test query. Fields marked false will cause query failures at execution time.

fields.validation_note: Must contain parent process executable image path
Must contain spawned child process image path
Confirm TELEMETRY_PROCESS is a valid eventName value in this tenant's schema
Confirm field is consistently populated by your deployed sensor type
Confirm time range filter bracket syntax matches your Search App version
Confirm TELEMETRY_CONNECTION is a valid eventName value
Same as Q-001
Must contain destination IP address string for NOT exclusion of INTERNAL_IP_RANGES
Must represent destination port number — used to filter for known C2 ports
Same as Q-001

fields.tenant_field_name: <REPLACE: objectCmd OR commandLine OR processCmd — validate exact name>
fields.validation_note: CRITICAL — field name varies across sensor types and tenant configurations. Validate before use. Wrong field name returns silent zero results, not an error.
Same as Q-001
Same as Q-001
TELEMETRY_PROCESS_ACCESS requires process access auditing enabled at sensor configuration level. Confirm this event type appears in your tenant before relying on this query.
Must refer to target process path (lsass.exe) — distinct from the accessing process
Must contain accessing process path — used for exclusion filtering of known-legitimate LSASS accessors
Same as Q-001

Q-004.telemetry_dependency_note: If TELEMETRY_PROCESS_ACCESS events are absent from your tenant: T-003 cannot confirm or deny LSASS access. Document the telemetry gap before proceeding.

fields.tenant_field_name: <REPLACE: objectHashId OR fileHash OR objectFileHashSha256 — validate exact name>
fields.validation_note: CRITICAL — field name varies across tenants. Wrong field name causes Q-005 to return zero results regardless of payload presence, invalidating the clean-state verification.
Confirm TELEMETRY_FILE is a valid eventName for file system events in this tenant
Same as Q-001
Confirm riskLevel is populated by your endpoint sensor and searchable in XDR Search App. Value set (high, critical) may vary by sensor type and configuration — validate exact values used.
Same as Q-001
Same as Q-001
Used for post-release window scoping — confirm bracket syntax for open-ended future window

activation_checklist.item: All tenant_config values replaced — no remaining <REPLACE:...> placeholders
All feature_flags set to reflect actual tenant capability — not defaulted to false without verification
All query_field_validation fields validated with live test query — validated: true for all fields
All required_permissions confirmed granted to the operator account for this incident
APPROVAL_IR_LEAD and APPROVAL_IR_MANAGER confirmed reachable at current time
AFFECTED_ENDPOINTS populated from Workbench Impact Scope (D-001) before first containment action
TIME_WINDOW_START and TIME_WINDOW_END set from Workbench incident timeline
Playbook activation documented in Workbench incident record with timestamp and operator identity
